They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\\n\\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\\n\\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \\\"\\\"` branch assigns\\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \\\"\\\"` branch: the\\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\\n  value is silently discarded.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \\\"\\\"` branch routes the\\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\\n`{ModelPin: \\\"qwen/qwen3.6\\\"}`.\\n\\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\\n  way to force a re-probe in tests.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\\nthe catalog:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\\n  discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \\\"\\\" && req.ModelRef == \\\"\\\" && r.Discovery == nil`.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\\n  `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\\n  the requested model.\\n\\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\\n  viability gate. Checks:\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\\n\\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\\nlevel support. That is Gap #6.\\n\\n## Layer 5 — Fuzzy matching\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:66` — `FuzzyMatchModel(input)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:70` — delegates to `fuzzyMatchInPool` using\\n  `d.AllModels()` (a flat deduplicated pool across all providers — not per-provider).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` — `fuzzyMatchInPool(input, pool)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:78` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:82` — exact-match short-circuit.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:89` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:96` — prefix match: candidate `m` is considered if `len(m) > len(input) && m[:len(input)] == input`. **The comparison is case-sensitive and treats slashes literally (Gap #3).**\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:106` — tiebreak: shortest suffix wins; alphabetical on equal suffix length. **Not version-aware (Gap #5).**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\nPool likely contains `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Input `qwen/qwen3.6` fails the\\nexact check, then fails the prefix check because `Q != q` and the slash is not\\nin the pool entries. The function returns `(\\\"\\\", false)`. `resolveNativeAgentProvider`\\nfalls through to the `isOpenRouterModel` heuristic at\\n`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:544` (vendor/model format → openrouter),\\nwhich is not what the operator wanted.\\n\\n## Layer 6 — Scoring\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` — `scoreCandidate(profile, plan)` entry.\\n  Returns a `float64`; higher is better.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:478` — base score `100`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:485` — `switch profile` over `cheap`, `standard`, `smart`, default.\\n  - `cheap` (lines 486–493): `local` +40, within-quota subscription +20, penalize `cr * 30`.\\n  - `standard` (lines 495–502): `local` +25, within-quota +15, penalize `cr * 10`.\\n  - `smart` (lines 504–512): `cr * 20` bonus (expensive > cheap), within-quota +5.\\n  - default (lines 514–522): treated as standard.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:525` — quota >80%: penalty scales with over-usage.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:528` — quota state unknown: −3.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:531` — routing-signal freshness: `cached` −1, `stale` −4.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:542` — historical success rate (≥3 samples):\\n  ≥0.8 +20, <0.5 −30.\\n\\n**No provider-affinity bonus (Gap #4).** If two harnesses both report viable\\nfor the same requested model, `scoreCandidate` does not look at\\n`plan.Provider` vs the provider the model was discovered on.\\n\\n## Layer 7 — Candidate ranking and selection\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:434` — `RankCandidates(profile, plans)`.\\n  - Assigns scores (line 444) or `-1` for non-viable (line 441).\\n  - Stable sort (lines 448–466): viable before non-viable; descending score;\\n    tiebreak prefers local-cost candidates, then alphabetical harness name.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:556` — `SelectBestCandidate(plans)` returns\\n  the first viable plan, or `\\\"no viable harness candidate: all harnesses rejected\\\"`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:567` — `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans` is the\\n  full-probe wrapper used by actual dispatch (it calls live `ProbeHarnessState`\\n  per harness before building plans).\\n\\n## Layer 8 — Dispatch\\n\\n`Runner.Run` is the dispatch fork:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:141` — `Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:148` — `resolveHarness(opts)` picks the harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:376` — `resolveHarness(opts)` definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382` — **Gap #2 origin**: if `opts.Harness == \\\"\\\"` and\\n  `opts.Provider != \\\"\\\"`, the `--provider` value is looked up as a harness\\n  alias. If it matches a registered harness (e.g. `agent`, `local`), that\\n  harness is chosen; otherwise `resolveHarness` falls back to `Config.Harness`.\\n  This is a parallel path that bypasses `NormalizeRouteRequest` entirely.\\n\\nDispatch branches (in order):\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:165` — `harnessName == \\\"virtual\\\"` → `RunVirtual(opts)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:170` — `harnessName == \\\"agent\\\"` → `RunAgent(opts)` (embedded).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:175` — `harnessName == \\\"script\\\"` → `RunScript(opts)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:187` — HTTP-provider harnesses with no binary (`lmstudio`, `openrouter`): surfaces the harness name as `opts.Provider` and delegates to `RunAgent`. The comment on lines 185–186 references `ddx-501e87ef` — the bug where cheap-tier attempts were burning <1s with \\\"exec: no command\\\".\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:278` — default exec path: `Executor.ExecuteInDir(ctx, harness.Binary, args, stdin, execDir)`.\\n\\nEmbedded agent dispatch:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:65` — `RunAgent(opts)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:94` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, opts.Provider, opts.ModelRef)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:464` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:493` — `resolveNativeAgentProvider(workDir, model, explicitProvider, explicitModelRef)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` — **Gap #2 again**: `explicitProvider != \\\"\\\"` overrides the default provider with the raw `opts.Provider` string. This is where `--provider vidar-omlx` finally re-enters the flow, but by now the routing engine has already ranked candidates without knowing about it.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` through `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:548` — discovery-based provider resolution: if the model isn't in the catalog and discovery has results, route to the first provider that advertises the model (exact or fuzzy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:555` — `cfg.BuildProviderWithOverrides(providerName, overrides)` materializes the provider object from the native agent config.\\n\\n## Layer 9 — Wire-level HTTP request\\n\\nThe embedded agent library builds the request body and dispatches to the\\nprovider endpoint. DDx hands off at `agentlib.Run`.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:127` — system prompt construction via\\n  `prompt.NewFromPreset(agentCfg.Preset).WithTools(tools).WithContextFiles(...).WithWorkDir(wd).Build()`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:153` — `agentlib.Request` assembly:\\n  `Prompt`, `SystemPrompt`, `Provider`, `Tools`, `MaxIterations`, `WorkDir`,\\n  `Metadata`, `Compactor`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:320` — `agentResult, err := agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`\\n  — handoff into the `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` library. DDx does not\\n  own the HTTP transport from here on.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:688` — `buildAgentProvider(cfg)` creates\\n  the provider used by the request. Branches:\\n  - `openai-compat` or `openai` (line 691): `oai.New(oai.Config{BaseURL, APIKey, Model})`.\\n  - `anthropic` (line 697): `anthropic.New(anthropic.Config{APIKey, Model})`.\\n  - `virtual` (line 702): `virtual.New(virtual.Config{DictDir})`.\\n\\nFor `vidar-omlx`, the native-config resolution at `resolveNativeAgentProvider`\\nloads the provider block from `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` or\\n`.agent/config.yaml`. The HTTP layer is OpenAI-compatible; the request body is\\nthe standard `/v1/chat/completions` payload built inside the agent library,\\nwith tool schemas appended.\\n\\nThe agent library exposes its wire-level dump via the `AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE`\\nenvironment variable (introduced in ddx-agent v0.3.12 — see ddx-a0f7a4bb).\\n\\n## Layer 10 — Response parsing\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:320` — same line; `agentlib.Run` returns\\n  `(AgentResult, error)` and DDx inspects the result starting at line 323.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:336` through `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:349` — maps\\n  `agentResult.Tokens`, `agentResult.ToolCalls`, `agentResult.SelectedProvider`,\\n  etc. into DDx `Result`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:355` — wall-clock deadline → error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:358` — idle timeout → error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:361` — compaction-stuck circuit breaker →\\n  error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:364` — stall detector (30 consecutive\\n  read-only tool calls) → error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:367` — generic agent error, including the\\n  `ErrProviderRequestTimeout` wrapper (line 369).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:380` — agent non-success status (e.g.\\n  `StatusCancelled`).\\n\\nThe `unexpected end of JSON input` error reported in ddx-6a5dfe35 surfaces\\nfrom inside the agent library's streaming decoder; DDx sees it via the generic\\nerror branch at line 367 and forwards the message unchanged. .ddx/executions/20260411T195303-5b6c0922/manifest.json:14:    "description": "ddx agent execute-loop fails on eitri: agent harness routes to z-ai/glm-5.1 ignoring local vidar config\n\nddx agent execute-loop consistently fails on the helix machine (eitri) with provider-level errors.\nThe execute-loop correctly selects and claims beads, but the agent harness never runs the work.\nCRITICAL: ddx agent run --harness agent works perfectly on the same machine, pointing to a\nmodel-resolution bug specifically in execute-bead/execute-loop.\n\nPROOF: direct agent run works, execute-loop fails:\n  ddx agent run --text \"Hello\" --harness agent --timeout 60s → succeeds with qwen3.5-27b on vidar\n  ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent → fails with z-ai/glm-5.1 via OpenRouter 404\n\nROOT CAUSE: Model resolution in execute-bead injects --model z-ai/glm-5.1, overriding the\nnative ~/.config/agent/config.yaml provider config (default_provider: vidar, model: qwen3.5-27b).\nThe z-ai/glm-5.1 model is not defined in models.yaml or the native config — it appears to come\nfrom stale execute-bead routing metadata. Even explicit --model flags on execute-loop don't\npropagate correctly to the spawned execute-bead subprocess.\n\nPROVIDER STATUS on eitri:\n- vidar (http://vidar:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): WORKS — confirmed with ddx agent run\n- bragi (http://bragi:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused\n- grendel (http://grendel:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused\n- openrouter (https://openrouter.ai/api/v1): FAILS — 404 guardrail restriction on claude-haiku\n\nMACHINE CONTEXT (eitri, Linux):\n- Hostname: eitri, paths use /home/erik/ and linuxbrew\n- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml defines providers with default_provider: vidar\n- models.yaml defines code-high profile → agent.openai: gpt-5.4 (OpenRouter surface)\n- No ~/.ddx.yml config file\n- No project-level .agent/config.yaml\n- Codex and claude harnesses nearly out of quota — reserved for review, not bulk execution\n- execute-loop was working previously (existing ddx beads show successful runs)\n- z-ai/glm-5.1 string appears nowhere in source — comes from runtime routing metadata\n\nENVIRONMENT VARIABLES: No AGENT_PROVIDER, AGENT_BASE_URL, AGENT_API_KEY, or AGENT_MODEL set.\nAll routing comes from native ~/.config/agent/config.yaml.\n\nIMPACT: ddx agent execute-loop is the primary execution surface for HELIX beads. If the only\nworking provider (vidar → qwen3.5-27b) cannot be used from execute-loop, entire queues drain\nas failures. The loop records each with retry_after (6 hours) and retries indefinitely.\n\nSUGGESTED FIXES:\n1. Fix execute-bead model resolution: respect native .agent/config.yaml provider config or\n   properly propagate the --model flag from execute-loop → execute-bead\n2. Add provider health checks: ddx agent doctor should test the actual provider with a\n   minimal API call, not just check binary existence\n3. Fail open on provider errors: when the default provider fails, fall back to the next\n   available provider in config rather than hard-failing\n4. Guardrail error clarity: surface OpenRouter 404 as \"provider unavailable due to\n   account restrictions\" rather than generic API error\n5. Execute-loop resilience: when all providers fail, mark bead truly blocked instead of\n   infinite retry_after cycles\n\nFILES INVOLVED:\n- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go (resolveNativeAgentProvider, resolveAgentConfig, resolveModel)\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go (execute-loop model flag propagation)\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead model override logic)\n- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml (machine config with vidar provider)",

.ddx/executions/20260411T195437-e3b15adb/manifest.json:14:    "description": "\u003cddx agent execute-loop injects z-ai/glm-5.1 model override that fails, while ddx agent run --harness agent works fine with qwen3.5-27b via vidar LMStudio host.\u003e\n\nddx agent execute-loop on eitri consistently fails because the execute-loop → execute-bead path injects --model z-ai/glm-5.1, overriding both the ~/.config/agent/config.yaml default_provider (vidar → qwen3.5-27b) AND the models.yaml catalog. The same agent harness works perfectly with direct ddx agent run.\n\nPROOF:\n\n1. dd agent run --text \"Hello\" --harness agent --timeout 60s succeeds:\n   - model: qwen3.5-27b\n   - exit_code: 0\n   - duration_ms: ~44s\n\n2. ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent fails:\n   - ps shows spawned process with --model z-ai/glm-5.1\n   - result: execution_failed, 502 Bad Gateway\n\n3. ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent --model qwen3.5-27b also fails:\n   - Still gets a 404 from OpenRouter or 502 from Alibaba\n   - execute-loop's --model flag doesn't propagate to the spawned execute-bead subprocess the way expected\n\nROOT CAUSE: execute-bead resolves the model from ddx metadata/routing (profiles, targets, or cached model catalog) rather than from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml or from execute-loop's --model flag. The z-ai/glm-5.1 model appears to come from a stale or incorrect routing data source.\n\nEVIDENCE:\n- models.yaml defines code-high profile with agent.openai: gpt-5.4\n- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml defines vidar with qwen3.5-27b\n- Neither source mentions z-ai/glm-5.1\n- The model appears to come from a separate routing layer in execute-bead or execute-loop (possibly a cached catalog entry or the server's worker routing config)\n\nFILES:\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go (execute-loop model resolution)\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead model resolution)  \n- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go (resolveModel chain)",

The real number of projects on this machine is single-digit. Root cause: the per-CLI-invocation auto-registration in serverreg.TryRegisterAsync registers the project on EVERY ddx command run with no dedupe, AND historical bugs (CWD drift in linked worktrees, the stale beads-store-write bug fixed in 26c0266) caused thousands of phantom 'projects' to land with paths like /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.claude/worktrees/agent-XYZ. The registry has no GC, no path canonicalization, no reachability check.\\n\\nFix scope:\\n1. Path canonicalization on register: filepath.Abs + EvalSymlinks before storing, so /tmp and /Users/erik/Projects/.... are normalized\\n2. Detect linked-worktree paths (via git rev-parse --git-common-dir) and store the PRIMARY worktree path, never the linked worktree path itself — this is the same fix we did for FindNearestDDxWorkspace in commit 26c0266 but applied to project registration\\n3. Dedupe on register: if a project with the same canonical path already exists, update its last_seen instead of creating a new entry\\n4. Reachability sweep: a periodic background goroutine (or a manual ddx server projects gc command) that stat()'s each registered project's path and either (a) marks it as 'unreachable' with a tombstone timestamp or (b) removes entries whose path has been gone for \u003e24h\\n5. Migration: on server startup, run dedupe + path canonicalization + reachability sweep ONCE against the existing state file so the eitri 4186 entries collapse to the real count\\n6. GET /api/projects gains a ?include_unreachable= query (default false) so the UI can hide tombstones unless asked","events_attachment":"ddx-23395b6a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T09:27:21.737933796Z","id":"ddx-23395b6a","issue_type":"bug","owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-756a1fa","status":"closed","title":"Server: dedupe and garbage-collect the project registry; tombstone unreachable projects","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:37.260925452Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:193:{"acceptance":"(1) A projectMiddleware or projectResolver extracts {project} from /api/projects/{project}/... routes and injects resolved ProjectEntry into request context. (2) Document routes (list, read, write, search), bead routes (list, show, create, update, claim, unclaim, reopen, ready, blocked, status, deps), and doc graph routes (graph, stale, deps, dependents, history, diff, show, write) are all registered under /api/projects/{project}/... in addition to existing legacy routes. (3) Legacy unscoped routes continue to work when exactly one project is registered (singleton compatibility). (4) cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server -run 'TestProject' -count=1 passes with at least one test covering scoped route resolution.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"f274719cb53dbdf67f3f8bf507bd3ebedf378dbf","created_at":"2026-04-16T19:35:21.69126362Z","description":"## Scope\nAdd project-scoping middleware to the DDx server and migrate all document, bead, and doc-graph HTTP routes to canonical project-scoped paths.\n\n## Context\nThe server currently registers ~50 routes under /api/... without project scoping. FEAT-002 requires /api/projects/{project}/... as canonical. Three worker routes already use this pattern (handleProjectWorkerList, handleProjectWorkerShow, handleProjectWorkerProgress) — follow that pattern.\n\n## Implementation approach\n1. Create a projectMiddleware (or helper function) that:\n   - Extracts {project} from the URL path\n   - Resolves it via GetProjectByID() or GetProjectByPath()\n   - Injects the resolved ProjectEntry into request context\n   - Returns 404 if project not found\n2. Register all document, bead, and doc-graph routes under /api/projects/{project}/...\n3. Keep legacy /api/... routes as singleton compatibility aliases that auto-resolve to the single registered project (error if \u003e1 project registered without scoping)\n4. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:198:{"acceptance":"1. **Root cause #1 fixed:** `attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}` is moved to AFTER the pre-claim hook succeeds. Regression test in `execute_bead_loop_test.go` asserts: with a fake `PreClaimHook` that fails on the first call and succeeds thereafter, the loop processes the same bead on the second iteration (not skipped because of stale `attempted` state).\n\n2. **Root cause #2 fixed:** the post-`Execute` outcome-handling block (lines ~510-862) does not exit the loop on transient `Store.*` errors. Each `return result, err` in that block is converted to: log the error, emit a `kind:loop-error` bead event when possible, set the bead to a short cooldown, and `continue` to the next iteration. The only exception is context cancellation (`ctx.Err()`), which still terminates immediately.\n\n3. **Regression tests for AC-2:** for each `Store.*` call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount, adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path, late AppendEvent), a test injects a transient error from a fake store and asserts: (a) the loop continues, (b) a `kind:loop-error` event is recorded with the failing operation name, (c) `result.Failures++` advances.\n\n4. **End-to-end reproduction guard:** an integration test seeds a bead.jsonl with 3 ready beads. The first returns `no_changes` (executor fake), the second returns `success`, the third returns `already_satisfied`. The loop runs to completion (all three processed) without exiting prematurely. With the pre-fix loop, this test fails on the first bead.\n\n5. **Worker-state semantics preserved:** existing tests that assert worker.State transitions (`running` → `exited` / `failed` / `stopped` / `reaped`) still pass. The fix changes loop *advancement*, not terminal-state classification.\n\n6. **Documented behavior:** `docs/agents/execute-loop.md` (or the closest existing doc) gains a \"loop termination\" section listing the only conditions that terminate the loop: context cancellation, `Once == true`, `PollInterval \u003c= 0` AND `nextCandidate` returns no candidate, `RoutePreflight` rejected. Explicitly states that Store errors during outcome handling do NOT terminate the loop.\n\n7. **Operator workaround retired:** the README's \"if `ddx work` exits early, restart it\" guidance (or equivalent) is removed; that workaround was masking this bug.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"1495ba706ffdc0e7bfb4833487ed068ffe732aeb","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:38.552407002Z","description":"## Symptom\n\n`ddx work` (no `--once` flag, no `--poll-interval`, default mode) exits after processing exactly one bead instead of draining the queue. Reproduced four times today on `/home/erik/Projects/agent` with `--harness lmstudio` pinned (which sidesteps the unrelated `ddx-c7081f89` server-worker tier-walk issue):\n\n| Run | Outcome on first bead | Worker terminal state | Queue still non-empty? |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| 2 | `already_satisfied` (`agent-4aa3584e` adjudicated satisfied) | `already_satisfied` / `exited` | yes — 2 ready beads remained |\n| 3 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |\n| 4 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |\n| 5 | `no_changes` from agent (`agent-6c7a4c11` declined as epic; bead left **claimed**) | `exited`, attempts=1 | yes — 5 ready beads remained |\n\nIn all four runs the loop should have continued to the next ready bead. Per `agent/.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, only `success`/`already_satisfied` close the bead and `no_changes`/`land_conflict`/`*_failed` unclaim and continue. None of these outcomes is documented or expected to be loop-terminal.\n\n## Root cause #1 (verified)\n\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:382`:\n\n```go\nattempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}\n\n// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.\n// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop\n// continues (ctx is not cancelled).\nif runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {\n    if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {\n        // ... log + emit ...\n        continue\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe bead is added to `attempted` **before** the pre-claim hook runs. On hook failure (e.g. diverged branch), the loop `continue`s but the bead stays in `attempted`. Next iteration, `nextCandidate()` (line 924) skips the still-ready bead because it's in `attempted`, returns `ok=false`, and the loop exits at line 374 because `PollInterval \u003c= 0`. Single-shot exit with `attempts=0`.\n\n**Reproduces:** runs 3 and 4 above.\n\n## Root cause #2 (suspected — needs targeted instrumentation)\n\nRuns 2 and 5 reached `Execute()` and got a real report back. Worker log shows the entry line `▶ \u003cbead\u003e: \u003ctitle\u003e` but no closing `✓ \u003cbead\u003e → \u003cresult\u003e` line (which is written at line 909). That means the loop exited somewhere between line 488 (`Executor.Execute`) and line 909 (the final summary log).\n\nBetween those bounds, **at least 16 distinct `return result, err` points** trigger if any `Store.*` operation returns an error: `Unclaim` (711, 700), `IncrNoChangesCount` (717), `adjudicateNoChanges` (721), `CloseWithEvidence` (520, 615, 694, 739), `Reopen` (635, 669), `SetExecutionCooldown` (749, 791, 803, 835, 844), late `AppendEvent` (862). Any single transient store error during outcome handling kills the worker.\n\nFor run 5 specifically, `agent-6c7a4c11` is now `Status: in_progress` with `Owner: ddx, Machine: sindri`. The bead was **never unclaimed** despite the agent returning `no_changes` — meaning the loop exited on or before line 711 (`w.Store.Unclaim`) failing. Investigation should look at what error `Unclaim` would return when called against a bead the same worker claimed seconds earlier; possible candidates: tracker-write race with concurrent worker, bead.jsonl re-write during a fetch by the pre-claim hook on a later iteration that already happened (no — pre-claim is pre-claim, not post-execute), or a malformed bead row that fails store-side validation.\n\nIndependent of the specific store error: the structural pattern of \"any Store.* error → exit the entire loop\" is the wrong default. A store error on outcome handling should at most park the bead and continue to the next, never terminate the worker.\n\n## Why this is loadbearing\n\nThe loop is the queue-drain primitive. Every observed failure-to-drain in the last 24 hours of operator complaints traces back to this — beads that should have been the worker's second/third/fourth attempt never got picked up because the worker exited after one attempt, and the operator manually restarts the drain (sometimes after a long delay). The visible queue depth is misleading: half the \"ready\" beads are sitting behind a worker that quietly died.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — fixes for both root causes\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go` — regression tests for each path\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Anything in the `agent` SDK (`/home/erik/Projects/agent`). The loop and all its store interactions are owned by ddx.\n- The pre-claim hook's divergence-detection logic itself — that's correct behavior; the bug is how the loop reacts to the hook's signal.\n- Adaptive min-tier / tier-walk machinery (`ddx-c7081f89`, `ddx-aec9d68c`) — independent.\n\n## Anti-goals\n\n- Do **not** swallow store errors silently. They must be surfaced (event + log) but should not kill the worker on transient/per-bead failures.\n- Do **not** convert the loop's Once/PollInterval semantics — they're correct.\n- Do **not** change worker terminal-state semantics (`exited`/`failed`/`stopped`/`reaped`).","events_attachment":"ddx-37cdb43a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-37cdb43a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-loop","kind:bug","phase:fix"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff only contains execution evidence (manifest.json + result.json) under .ddx/executions/. No changes to execute_bead_loop.go, tests, or docs are visible, so none of the 7 acceptance criteria can be evaluated as implemented.","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-842ed336","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: ddx work exits after one bead instead of draining the queue","updated_at":"2026-04-30T00:04:15.728726551Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:201:{"acceptance":"Bead read surfaces resolve one current state per bead ID from the append-only tracker; `ddx bead show` returns the latest row; `ready`/`blocked`/status surfaces do not surface stale or duplicate bead states; and regression tests cover repeated updates for the same ID","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a2db669db91cca67f9316b854c5768963a8ee304","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:27:35.157202615Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nReview area: append-only tracker state resolution. Evidence covers the current .ddx/beads.jsonl containing multiple rows for the same bead ID after updates/events, `ddx bead ready` surfacing duplicate IDs, and `ddx bead show ddx-40a722c3 --json` returning an older open row even though a later closed row for the same bead exists in the tracker.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nMake the bead store resolve the latest row for each bead ID consistently across all read surfaces.\n\n## Scope\n- Reproduce stale-state resolution across `show`, `ready`, `blocked`, and other derived queue/status surfaces\n- Ensure append-only tracker rows are folded to the latest effective bead state per ID before any queue derivation or display\n- Preserve events/history while making the live bead view unique and current\n- Add regression coverage for repeated updates to the same bead ID\n\n## Boundaries\n- Do not change bead mutation semantics or abandon append-only evidence\n- This is about latest-state resolution and queue correctness, not reprioritization policy","id":"ddx-38d8d737","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:cli","area:bead"],"notes":"Fixed in a2db669: append-only tracker rows are folded to latest effective state across show/ready/blocked surfaces.","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-004","status":"closed","title":"Use latest bead state across show/ready/blocked surfaces","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:19:08.806064926Z"}

Test covers the multi-binary-on-PATH scenario by shadowing a fake ddx in a temp dir earlier on PATH.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T03:08:24.081395593Z","description":"install.sh installs the ddx binary to $HOME/.local/bin/ddx as the canonical location. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:280:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:39:31.542862754Z","description":"Finding from fresh-eyes review of the run/try/work boundary plan.\n\nProblem:\nThe plan says there should be one simple run command layered under try and work, but current governing docs and shipped skill references still describe ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, execute-loop, quorum harness flags, and agent-provider command surfaces as active user/automation primitives. Examples found during review include FEAT-006 Agent Service overview/responsibilities, FEAT-010 Layer 1 wording, and cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md persona/quorum/help examples. Parallel copied skill docs under .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx contain the same stale vocabulary.\n\nWhy this matters:\nThe docs are executable guidance for agents. Leaving legacy command names and flags in skills will keep agents routing work through the old surfaces even if the implementation adds top-level run/try/work.\n\nRequired fix:\nMake the public command taxonomy bright-line and uniform across feature docs, skill docs, and CLI help: ddx run is the single agent execution primitive, ddx try layers retry/review policy over run, and ddx work layers bead orchestration over try. Agent-internal service commands may exist only as hidden/internal plumbing and must not be documented as workflow commands.\n\nAcceptance:\n- FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 no longer describe ddx agent run/execute-bead/execute-loop as public orchestration commands.\n- DDx skill references in cli/internal/skills/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and .claude/skills/ddx use ddx run/try/work consistently.\n- Quorum/compare/persona examples are either migrated to the simplified surface or removed if they imply DDx harness routing.\n- A docs/skill grep test or validation step fails on stale public references to ddx agent run, execute-bead, execute-loop, --harnesses, or provider-routing examples outside explicit migration notes.","id":"ddx-52ce6249","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:docs","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-deb9be7e","priority":1,"spec_id":"review:power-passthrough-boundary-stale-commands","status":"closed","title":"review finding: remove stale ddx agent run and execute-bead command guidance","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:50:03.186901956Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:289:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-11T19:53:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2860272","created_at":"2026-04-11T19:33:05.943045249Z","description":"ddx agent execute-loop fails on eitri: agent harness routes to z-ai/glm-5.1 ignoring local vidar config\n\nddx agent execute-loop consistently fails on the helix machine (eitri) with provider-level errors.\nThe execute-loop correctly selects and claims beads, but the agent harness never runs the work.\nCRITICAL: ddx agent run --harness agent works perfectly on the same machine, pointing to a\nmodel-resolution bug specifically in execute-bead/execute-loop.\n\nPROOF: direct agent run works, execute-loop fails:\n  ddx agent run --text \"Hello\" --harness agent --timeout 60s → succeeds with qwen3.5-27b on vidar\n  ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent → fails with z-ai/glm-5.1 via OpenRouter 404\n\nROOT CAUSE: Model resolution in execute-bead injects --model z-ai/glm-5.1, overriding the\nnative ~/.config/agent/config.yaml provider config (default_provider: vidar, model: qwen3.5-27b).\nThe z-ai/glm-5.1 model is not defined in models.yaml or the native config — it appears to come\nfrom stale execute-bead routing metadata. Even explicit --model flags on execute-loop don't\npropagate correctly to the spawned execute-bead subprocess.\n\nPROVIDER STATUS on eitri:\n- vidar (http://vidar:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): WORKS — confirmed with ddx agent run\n- bragi (http://bragi:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused\n- grendel (http://grendel:1234/v1, qwen3.5-27b): DOWN — connection refused\n- openrouter (https://openrouter.ai/api/v1): FAILS — 404 guardrail restriction on claude-haiku\n\nMACHINE CONTEXT (eitri, Linux):\n- Hostname: eitri, paths use /home/erik/ and linuxbrew\n- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml defines providers with default_provider: vidar\n- models.yaml defines code-high profile → agent.openai: gpt-5.4 (OpenRouter surface)\n- No ~/.ddx.yml config file\n- No project-level .agent/config.yaml\n- Codex and claude harnesses nearly out of quota — reserved for review, not bulk execution\n- execute-loop was working previously (existing ddx beads show successful runs)\n- z-ai/glm-5.1 string appears nowhere in source — comes from runtime routing metadata\n\nENVIRONMENT VARIABLES: No AGENT_PROVIDER, AGENT_BASE_URL, AGENT_API_KEY, or AGENT_MODEL set.\nAll routing comes from native ~/.config/agent/config.yaml.\n\nIMPACT: ddx agent execute-loop is the primary execution surface for HELIX beads. If the only\nworking provider (vidar → qwen3.5-27b) cannot be used from execute-loop, entire queues drain\nas failures. The loop records each with retry_after (6 hours) and retries indefinitely.\n\nSUGGESTED FIXES:\n1. Fix execute-bead model resolution: respect native .agent/config.yaml provider config or\n   properly propagate the --model flag from execute-loop → execute-bead\n2. Add provider health checks: ddx agent doctor should test the actual provider with a\n   minimal API call, not just check binary existence\n3. Fail open on provider errors: when the default provider fails, fall back to the next\n   available provider in config rather than hard-failing\n4. Guardrail error clarity: surface OpenRouter 404 as \"provider unavailable due to\n   account restrictions\" rather than generic API error\n5. Execute-loop resilience: when all providers fail, mark bead truly blocked instead of\n   infinite retry_after cycles\n\nFILES INVOLVED:\n- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go (resolveNativeAgentProvider, resolveAgentConfig, resolveModel)\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go (execute-loop model flag propagation)\n- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead model override logic)\n- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml (machine config with vidar provider)","id":"ddx-57388aff","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:routing"],"notes":"Duplicate of ddx-c1822c89. Same root cause: execute-loop model routing ignores native harness config and picks catalog-tier model instead of locally-configured endpoints.","owner":"ddx","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"execute-loop provider failures on helix machine: OpenRouter guardrail 404 + Alibaba 502","updated_at":"2026-04-13T20:18:05.493220127Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:348:{"acceptance":"cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go ExecuteBead() contains ONLY: worktree setup (or accept pre-made worktree), prompt construction, agent harness dispatch, progress event emission, and a final completion event. No Merge, no UpdateRef, no preserveRef, no evaluateRequiredGates, no outcome classification beyond {task_succeeded, task_failed, task_no_changes}. All merge/preserve/gate logic moved to a new parent-side orchestrator that can be run interactively. Regression test: two workers on two different beads complete in arbitrary order; parent process merges both sequentially with interactive conflict resolution if needed; no worktree-internal merge code paths are exercised.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T05:36:33Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"49c3d2bda576630dd2f6555bc143082344f079ba","created_at":"2026-04-14T04:02:25.941816183Z","description":"The current execute-bead worker does WAY too much: creates a worktree, runs the agent, evaluates required gates, decides merge-by-default vs preserve vs land-conflict, writes preserve refs, synthesizes commits on IsDirty, classifies 7+ outcomes, manages heartbeats, handles claim/unclaim contention, runs recoverOrphans on stale worktrees. This surface is why (a) two concurrent workers race on the main-branch merge and the loser's good work ends up under refs/ddx/iterations/ needing manual rescue, (b) no_changes gets misclassified as synthesized-commit success, (c) the gate-enforcement path and the merge path are entangled, and (d) the worker needs special privileges (update-ref, merge, rebase) that a simple task-runner should never have.\\n\\nThe correct design is the one the operator just described: execute-bead is a thin worker. 'I have been handed a worktree and a task. I will execute that task and make commits in the worktree. I will stream progress events to ddx server. I will notify ddx server when I am done, reporting the worktree branch and the commit(s) I made. Then I exit.' That's it.\\n\\nEverything else — merge to main, conflict resolution, required-gate evaluation, preserve-under-ref, decide whether to close the bead — moves to the PARENT process (the operator, or a driver tool running interactively in the terminal). Merge conflicts are resolved interactively the same as any other merge. A human (or an outer agent with full git authority) reviews the agent's commits and decides what to do with them.\\n\\nThis simplification deletes most of ExecuteBead() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go and moves:\\n- resolveBase / baseRev tracking → parent\\n- Merge() call → parent\\n- preserveRef / UpdateRef / refs/ddx/iterations/ writes → parent\\n- evaluateRequiredGates → parent (or a separate 'ddx agent check' step)\\n- outcome classification 'merged/preserved/land-conflict/error' → parent\\n- recoverOrphans → parent (cleanup runs when parent next launches workers)\\n- heartbeatIsStale reclaim logic → parent (parent tracks worker health)\\n\\nWhat the worker keeps:\\n- Take a worktree dir + task description (prompt) as input\\n- Launch the agent harness with the prompt\\n- Stream progress events to ddx server (session log + structured JSONL)\\n- On exit, emit a final event: worktree path, branch, commit shas (or empty), exit code, duration\\n- That's it\\n\\nExecute-loop likewise shrinks or goes away. In the clean design, a driver loop in the parent process picks ready beads, creates a /tmp worktree per bead, calls 'ddx agent execute-bead' (worker-only mode), waits for the worker's exit event from ddx server, reviews the result, and merges or discards. When two beads are in flight concurrently, the parent serializes the merge step — no race possible.","events_attachment":"ddx-679d2fc7/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T06:18:33.856661655Z","id":"ddx-679d2fc7","issue_type":"task","owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-49c3d2b","status":"closed","title":"Simplify execute-bead worker: task-only, no merge/preserve/gate logic","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:51.136077845Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:373:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer opening the document graph, I want a structured integrity panel that groups real document problems by kind and points me at the files to fix, so I can triage a noisy graph quickly and confirm when it is healthy.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` (or a sibling `Issues` field) is a typed slice with `Kind`, `Path`, `ID`, `Message`, and optional `RelatedPath`. Kinds include at minimum: `duplicate_id`, `parse_error`, `missing_dep`, `id_path_missing`, `id_path_mismatch`, `required_root_missing`, `cascade_unknown`.\n2. GraphQL schema exposes `DocGraph.issues: [GraphIssue!]!` with the same fields. The existing string `warnings` either stays (rendered from issues) or is deprecated with a schema comment.\n3. Unit tests in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` cover each issue kind: fixtures that produce exactly one instance of that kind and assert the typed output.\n4. `/graph` page renders an **Integrity** panel when `issues.length \u003e 0`:\n   - grouped by `kind` with a count per group;\n   - each row shows path, id, and message;\n   - path links to the document viewer (reuses existing documents route);\n   - total-issues badge appears next to the page title.\n5. With zero issues, neither the panel nor the badge renders, and the graph view is visually identical to a healthy graph today.\n6. Duplicate-ID rows show both paths and a \"copy suggested unique ID\" button; the suggestion is deterministic (same input → same output) and a unit test asserts the suggestion function's stability.\n7. `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`) CLI command prints the same issue list grouped by kind, exits 0 if empty and 1 if any issues present. Integration test asserts exit codes and grouped output.\n8. Playwright e2e:\n   - navigates to `/graph` on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target;\n   - asserts the Integrity panel shows groups \"Duplicate ID (1)\" and \"Missing dep target (1)\";\n   - expands the Duplicate ID group and asserts both paths are visible;\n   - clicks t- CI guard tests (sibling bead handles)\n- Hygiene-bead AC TD-NNN substitution + migration survey (sibling bead handles)",

.ddx/executions/20260502T190445-bbf9f442/prompt.md:5:Authoring-only sub-task of the bead state-machine work. Children (filed separately) handle the mechanical reconciliation, CI guards, and housekeeping that follow this design.

This bead OUTPUTS the canonical TD doc that the 5 hygiene beads + the 3 sibling reconciliation beads reference. NO code or schema changes in this bead — those are separate sub-beads.

## Critical constraint (per ADR-004)

The bead-record schema MUST stay compatible with the bd/br interchange contract. Status enum is FIXED at the bd/br canonical 6 (open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in labels, events, or Extra map — NOT in new statuses.

## Today's actual state (verified)

- bead-record.schema.json enum: 6 values (the bd/br canonical set)
- FEAT-004 line 65 docs: 3 values (lags schema)
- Persisted reality: 'ddx bead list --json | jq -r .[].status | sort -u' shows only open/in_progress/closed in active rows today
- Code uses additional names (done, needs_human, pending, ready, review, needs_investigation) but these are NOT persisted statuses — event kinds, terminal phases, derived queue categories, or labels

## Five in-flight hygiene beads each touch state-machine vocabulary (must align with this TD)

- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): NoChangesContract subsection
- ddx-3c154349 (auto-triage): TriageContract subsection
- ddx-aede917d (drain pause on quota): QuotaPauseContract subsection
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): RateLimitRetryContract subsection
- ddx-da11a34a (lock contention): LockContentionContract subsection

## Scope (this bead authors the doc only)

Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-NNN-bead-state-machine.md with:

1. Distinguish category of every observed name as one of: persisted bead status (locked to bd/br set) / derived queue category / event kind / terminal phase / claim metadata / label / Extra metadata field / worker state.
2. Persisted status enumeration — confirm the bd/br-compatible 6-value set. Status enum is NOT modified.
3. Transition matrix — table or diagram: which status → which is allowed; who can drive each (operator-via-CLI, drain loop, agent, reviewer); what events fire on each transition.
4. Event vocabulary — unified across hygiene beads (no_changes_*, drain-paused-quota, etc.).
5. Outcome → label/event/Extra mapping — when execute-bead returns merged/already_satisfied/review_block/execution_failed/no_changes_*, the resulting bead state changes (status transition within the 6, label add/remove, event append, Extra field update).
6. Claim semantics — when claimed, when released, stale claim handling.
7. Naming-role decision matrix — every observed name labeled with role + rationale: blocked, in_progress, open, closed, cancelled, proposed (current schema 6), done, pending, ready, review (in code as non-status), needs_human (label today; stays label), needs_investigation (LABEL per bd/br compatibility, NOT status), blocked-on-upstream:<id> (label).
8. Per-hygiene-bead contract subsection — one per bead (NoChangesContract, TriageContract, QuotaPauseContract, RateLimitRetryContract, LockContentionContract): status transitions used (within canonical 6), labels added/removed, events fired, claim behavior, loop interactions.
9. Future-change process — explicit rule: any bead introducing or changing labels/events/outcome semantics/claim handling MUST cite the TD section authorizing it. Adding a new persisted status requires upstream bd/br coordination + ADR-004 amendment.
10. Worker-state enumeration — parallel section: idle / draining / paused-quota / paused-rate-limit / exiting. Worker-state is distinct from bead-state, owned by worker process not bead store.
11. Migration plan placeholder — say 'sibling bead handles survey + execution' (the actual survey runs in the sibling bead).

## What does NOT land here

- Schema/code/docs reconciliation (sibling bead handles)
- CI guard tests (sibling bead handles)
- Hygiene-bead AC TD-NNN substitution + migration survey (sibling bead handles)

.ddx/executions/20260502T191634-fc858450/prompt.md:5:Two related bugs causing 'ddx server workers list' (and other server-talking CLI commands) to fail with 'connection refused dial tcp 127.0.0.1:<stale-port>' even when a live server is running.

## Diagnosis

**writeAddrFile already exists** at cli/internal/server/server.go:287-312 and is called from line 273 on every startup. So the file IS written. The real bugs are:

**Bug A: No singleton-server guard.** Multiple 'ddx server' instances can start on the same machine concurrently. Each one calls writeAddrFile and overwrites the previous record. When one dies (e.g., a manual launch test), it leaves the address-file pointing to itself even though it's no longer running. systemd unit (Type=simple) prevents systemd-spawned duplicates of the same unit name but does NOT stop a manual 'ddx server' from racing.

Observed today: live systemd server PID 3442304 listening on 7743 (started ~14:27), but address file shows PID 3482077 / port 4174 (started ~15:01, died). Some manual or test launch overwrote the file then died.

**Bug B: CLI doesn't validate pid-alive before trusting the address file.** ReadServerAddr at cli/internal/server/server.go:328+ returns whatever's in the file. CLI commands then dial the recorded URL. If the recorded pid is dead, dial fails. No fallback to the systemd default (127.0.0.1:7743), no warning.

## Fix

Two halves, same surface (server bootstrap + CLI client read path):

**Server-side (singleton guard):**
- On startup, server acquires flock on ~/.local/share/ddx/server.lock
- If lock is held by an alive process: refuse to start with clear error ('ddx server already running, pid=<N>, addr=<URL>; only one ddx server per machine'). Exit non-zero.
- If lock is acquired (or stale — held by dead pid): proceed; release-on-exit handler clears the lock.
- writeAddrFile happens AFTER the lock acquires — so the address file always corresponds to the lock-holder.

**Client-side (defense in depth):**
- ReadServerAddr (or its callers) validates the recorded pid is alive (kill -0 equivalent on Unix, OpenProcess on Windows)
- If pid is dead: emit warning ('server.addr points to a dead pid <N>; falling back to default 127.0.0.1:7743') and use the default
- Optionally clear the stale file (best-effort; don't fail if unwritable)

## Out of scope

- Multi-server-per-machine support (the singleton guard is intentional — one server per machine is the design per FEAT-020)
- Federation: hub/spoke topology (Story 14) is multi-node, NOT multi-server-per-node

- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql: add 'sort: ArtifactSort' arg on Query.artifacts with enum values PATH_ASC|PATH_DESC|TITLE_ASC|TITLE_DESC|UPDATED_DESC|UPDATED_ASC|STALENESS, plus 'staleness: String' arg.
- Resolver: implement sort with deterministic (sortKey, id) tie-breaker; implement staleness filter; keep existing search (title+path substring, case-insensitive) unchanged in this bead.
- Frontend routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts: pass URL 'q' as the GraphQL 'search' arg instead of client-side substring filter on already-loaded edges. Drop the client-side fallback so search covers the full set.
- Frontend +page.svelte: when q/sort/mediaType/staleness change, reset 'after' cursor before reloading.

Out of scope: sort dropdown UI, staleness chip UI, URL state for new params, snippet rendering, body-text search, phase/prefix filters.

.ddx/executions/20260502T133823-f8df7c6d/prompt.md:5:Phase 1 plumbing for Story 6 artifact listing. See /tmp/story-6-final.md.

Changes:
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go: translate 'image/*'-style mediaType arg to a prefix match (HasPrefix on 'image/'); current code uses exact equality so the Images chip never matches image/png or image/svg+xml.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql: add 'sort: ArtifactSort' arg on Query.artifacts with enum values PATH_ASC|PATH_DESC|TITLE_ASC|TITLE_DESC|UPDATED_DESC|UPDATED_ASC|STALENESS, plus 'staleness: String' arg.
- Resolver: implement sort with deterministic (sortKey, id) tie-breaker; implement staleness filter; keep existing search (title+path substring, case-insensitive) unchanged in this bead.
- Frontend routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts: pass URL 'q' as the GraphQL 'search' arg instead of client-side substring filter on already-loaded edges. Drop the client-side fallback so search covers the full set.
- Frontend +page.svelte: when q/sort/mediaType/staleness change, reset 'after' cursor before reloading.

Out of scope: sort dropdown UI, staleness chip UI, URL state for new params, snippet rendering, body-text search, phase/prefix filters.

- Changing q/sort/mediaType/staleness clears 'after' cursor.

.ddx/executions/20260501T030628-7b39c14f/prompt.md:5:Fix the document dependency graph interactivity. Addresses US-081 AC which is specced but unimplemented.

Bugs to fix:
1. Pan/zoom sizing race: D3Graph.svelte uses el.clientWidth at init time which may be 0. Replace with ResizeObserver: set up simulation once observer fires with non-zero dimensions. This also handles window resize.
2. Node click = no handler: nodeGroup has mouseenter/mousemove/mouseleave but no onclick. Add click handler that navigates to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents/:path (for markdown artifacts) or /artifacts/:artifactId (for other types). Distinguish by mediaType from graph node data.

Viewport persistence:
- Save zoom transform (x, y, k) to component state or URL param (e.g. ?zoom=1.2&pan=120,45) on each zoom/pan end event.
- On Back navigation, restore the saved transform before the D3 effect runs.

New features:
- Filter chips on graph page: filter nodes by mediaType (markdown, image, unknown) and staleness (fresh, stale, missing). Hidden nodes removed from simulation; edges to hidden nodes removed.
- Node color = staleness: green=fresh, amber=stale, red=missing (not dependsOn.length as today).
- 'View document list' link on graph page → /documents (which redirects to /artifacts).
- 'View in Graph' link on artifact detail page (once ddx-d7d1119d exists) → /graph with node pre-selected.

IntegrityPanel path links: currently link to /documents/... — update to /artifacts/:artifactId once /artifacts route exists; keep /documents fallback until then.

.ddx/executions/20260430T190925-a1edeb1e/prompt.md:5:Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.

Backend mutation:
- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult
- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP
- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }
- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.
- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.

Auto-repairable strategies:
- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter
- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)
- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map

UI changes to IntegrityPanel:
- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)
- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change
- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)
- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast
- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback

Failure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).
.ddx/executions/20260430T190925-a1edeb1e/prompt.md:8:1. graphRepairIssue mutation in schema; resolver atomically applies structured edit and re-validates.
2. IntegrityPanel shows 'Preview fix' for REMOVE_MISSING_DEP and APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID issues.
3. Preview expands inline showing frontmatter diff.
4. Clicking 'Apply fix' calls mutation; on success issue disappears and graph refreshes.
5. File-changed error: inline error message in issue row; copy-snippet fallback unchanged.
6. Ambiguous or non-auto-repairable issues: no 'Preview fix' shown; copy-snippet only.
7. Go resolver test covers: successful repair, stale file hash rejection, path traversal rejection.

.ddx/executions/20260430T000220-82f59422/prompt.md:8:1. **Root cause #1 fixed:** `attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}` is moved to AFTER the pre-claim hook succeeds. Regression test in `execute_bead_loop_test.go` asserts: with a fake `PreClaimHook` that fails on the first call and succeeds thereafter, the loop processes the same bead on the second iteration (not skipped because of stale `attempted` state).

2. **Root cause #2 fixed:** the post-`Execute` outcome-handling block (lines ~510-862) does not exit the loop on transient `Store.*` errors. Each `return result, err` in that block is converted to: log the error, emit a `kind:loop-error` bead event when possible, set the bead to a short cooldown, and `continue` to the next iteration. The only exception is context cancellation (`ctx.Err()`), which still terminates immediately.

3. **Regression tests for AC-2:** for each `Store.*` call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount, adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path, late AppendEvent), a test injects a transient error from a fake store and asserts: (a) the loop continues, (b) a `kind:loop-error` event is recorded with the failing operation name, (c) `result.Failures++` advances.

4. **End-to-end reproduction guard:** an integration test seeds a bead.jsonl with 3 ready beads. The first returns `no_changes` (executor fake), the second returns `success`, the third returns `already_satisfied`. The loop runs to completion (all three processed) without exiting prematurely. With the pre-fix loop, this test fails on the first bead.

5. **Worker-state semantics preserved:** existing tests that assert worker.State transitions (`running` → `exited` / `failed` / `stopped` / `reaped`) still pass. The fix changes loop *advancement*, not terminal-state classification.

6. **Documented behavior:** `docs/agents/execute-loop.md` (or the closest existing doc) gains a "loop termination" section listing the only conditions that terminate the loop: context cancellation, `Once == true`, `PollInterval <= 0` AND `nextCandidate` returns no candidate, `RoutePreflight` rejected. Explicitly states that Store errors during outcome handling do NOT terminate the loop.

7. **Operator workaround retired:** the README's "if `ddx work` exits early, restart it" guidance (or equivalent) is removed; that workaround was masking this bug.

Scope of PRD update:

(1) Restructure Problem section into 6 clusters keyed to thesis principles in product-vision.md:
- Abstraction (no structure / no composition / no integrity guarantees / no transferability)
- Iteration over tracked work (no reusable work-item store / no reusable execution evidence)
- Methodology plurality (no reusable agent dispatch — every workflow tool reinvents)
- LLM physics (NEW cluster — token cost as a permanent constraint; nothing in current PRD)
- Evidence/provenance (no provenance for generated artifacts; no measurement; no feedback capture)
- Human-AI collaboration (no composition for handoffs; no discoverability; no network access)

Existing problem bullets re-grouped under these headers; add the LLM-physics cluster.

(2) Goals (primary): reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP goal.

(3) Non-goals: sharpen loop wording — DDx owns mechanical queue drain (ddx work); content-aware supervisory decisions remain plugin/HELIX. Add: 'DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers.'

(4) Feature blurbs: FEAT-001 (CLI: run/try/work top-level + ddx agent passthrough); FEAT-005 (multi-media identity); FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer + structural passthrough); FEAT-007 (sidecar + generated_by + separate staleness); FEAT-008/021 (media-type rendering + regenerate + layer-aware run views); FEAT-010 (3-layer architecture + substrate unification); FEAT-019 (child of FEAT-010 — evaluation UX, workflow shapes -> skills).

Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate; invocation upstream.

.ddx/executions/20260429T162702-2c963978/manifest.json:14:    "description": "execute-loop's adaptive min-tier promotion (escalation.AdaptiveMinTierThreshold etc.) silently skips cheap tier when trailing cheap-tier success rate falls under threshold. Today the only visible signal is a single log line:\n\n  adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate 0.00 over 9 attempts; threshold 0.20) — min-tier=standard\n\nThere is no documented or discoverable way to reset this state. Issues with the current design:\n\n1. STATE LOCATION IS NOT DOCUMENTED. The trailing-window metric is computed from .ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl per-project (and possibly other sources — rotating that file alone did NOT clear the 0% verdict in a recent test, so additional state may live elsewhere). A user who wants to reset cannot find authoritative documentation of where the state lives.\n\n2. NO RESET CLI. There is no `ddx agent route-status reset`, no `ddx agent metrics clear`, no `ddx agent doctor --reset-adaptive`. The only path is to edit/truncate JSONL files by hand, which is fragile and breaks compatibility with any future schema.\n\n3. SILENT NOISE. Every cheap-tier failure permanently affects future routing without surfacing to the user. If a configuration bug (e.g. the model_overrides issue tracked as ddx-5538aa5b) caused 9 immediate failures, the adaptive logic now blocks cheap tier even after the configuration is fixed. The user has no way to know 'this is stale state, not real performance signal' without reading source.\n\n4. MITIGATION VIA --no-adaptive-min-tier IS NOT A FIX, IT'S AN OVERRIDE. The flag bypasses the gating but also defeats the legitimate purpose of the metric. We need ability to RESET the metric and re-evaluate from a clean slate, not permanently disable the feature.\n\n5. THE THRESHOLD/WINDOW DEFAULTS ARE AGGRESSIVE. Trailing window of 9-50 attempts at threshold 0.20 means a single cheap-tier provider misconfiguration (which is easy to hit, see ddx-5538aa5b) condemns cheap-tier for many subsequent invocations. Meanwhile EVERY configuration error is recorded as cheap-tier failure even when the failure was 'no viable provider' rather than 'model produced bad output'.\n\nRequested behavior:\n\n- A first-class CLI: `ddx agent route-status reset [--scope cheap|all]` that clears the adaptive metric for the current project, with a confirm prompt by default.\n- A flag on `ddx agent doctor` that displays current adaptive state per tier (success rate, attempt count, threshold, current floor) so users can SEE the problem.\n- An option to record only EXECUTION outcomes (model produced output) in adaptive metrics, NOT no-viable-provider/configuration failures — those should be tracked separately and never count against a tier's reputation.\n- Documented location and schema for the metric store, so users who want to inspect or surgically edit understand the on-disk format.",
.ddx/executions/20260429T162702-2c963978/prompt.md:5:execute-loop's adaptive min-tier promotion (escalation.AdaptiveMinTierThreshold etc.) silently skips cheap tier when trailing cheap-tier success rate falls under threshold. Today the only visible signal is a single log line:

  adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate 0.00 over 9 attempts; threshold 0.20) — min-tier=standard

There is no documented or discoverable way to reset this state. Issues with the current design:

1. STATE LOCATION IS NOT DOCUMENTED. The trailing-window metric is computed from .ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl per-project (and possibly other sources — rotating that file alone did NOT clear the 0% verdict in a recent test, so additional state may live elsewhere). A user who wants to reset cannot find authoritative documentation of where the state lives.

2. NO RESET CLI. There is no `ddx agent route-status reset`, no `ddx agent metrics clear`, no `ddx agent doctor --reset-adaptive`. The only path is to edit/truncate JSONL files by hand, which is fragile and breaks compatibility with any future schema.

3. SILENT NOISE. Every cheap-tier failure permanently affects future routing without surfacing to the user. If a configuration bug (e.g. the model_overrides issue tracked as ddx-5538aa5b) caused 9 immediate failures, the adaptive logic now blocks cheap tier even after the configuration is fixed. The user has no way to know 'this is stale state, not real performance signal' without reading source.

4. MITIGATION VIA --no-adaptive-min-tier IS NOT A FIX, IT'S AN OVERRIDE. The flag bypasses the gating but also defeats the legitimate purpose of the metric. We need ability to RESET the metric and re-evaluate from a clean slate, not permanently disable the feature.

5. THE THRESHOLD/WINDOW DEFAULTS ARE AGGRESSIVE. Trailing window of 9-50 attempts at threshold 0.20 means a single cheap-tier provider misconfiguration (which is easy to hit, see ddx-5538aa5b) condemns cheap-tier for many subsequent invocations. Meanwhile EVERY configuration error is recorded as cheap-tier failure even when the failure was 'no viable provider' rather than 'model produced bad output'.

Requested behavior:

- A first-class CLI: `ddx agent route-status reset [--scope cheap|all]` that clears the adaptive metric for the current project, with a confirm prompt by default.
- A flag on `ddx agent doctor` that displays current adaptive state per tier (success rate, attempt count, threshold, current floor) so users can SEE the problem.
- An option to record only EXECUTION outcomes (model produced output) in adaptive metrics, NOT no-viable-provider/configuration failures — those should be tracked separately and never count against a tier's reputation.
- Documented location and schema for the metric store, so users who want to inspect or surgically edit understand the on-disk format.

.ddx/executions/20260429T185803-f3a78895/manifest.json:14:    "description": "FEAT-007 gap. HTTP has GET /api/docs/{id}/dependents but MCP has ddx_doc_deps (upstream direction only) with no reverse. Add ddx_doc_dependents MCP tool mirroring the HTTP endpoint. Agents doing cascade staleness analysis or impact-of-change reasoning rely on the reverse direction.",
.ddx/executions/20260429T175957-601d3ad4/manifest.json:13:    "title": "agent workers: stale 'running' entries can't be cleaned without server restart",
.ddx/executions/20260429T175957-601d3ad4/manifest.json:14:    "description": "When a local execute-bead/execute-loop process exits abnormally (killed, OOM, parent shell terminated, container crash) the worker entry can remain in the in-memory registry forever. `ddx agent workers` lists it as state=running with elapsed_ms continuously advancing, even though no PID exists.\n\nReproducing today (axon repo): worker local-20260429T030828-075a28c5 (bead hx-5f4d4ea1, harness=agent, model=qwen3.6) started at 03:08 UTC, has logged elapsed=10+ hours, and `ps -ef | grep execute-bead` shows nothing. The git worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-hx-5f4d4ea1-* has only the checkpoint commit (no actual work commits), confirming the worker died early.\n\nAvailable paths to clean up are all unsatisfying:\n1. `ddx agent workers stop --bead hx-5f4d4ea1` returns 'no matching workers' (because the matcher only finds live workers)\n2. `ddx agent workers stop \u003cworker-id\u003e` returns server error 400 'worker not running' (same contradiction — the registry says running, the stopper says not running)\n3. `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` works but kills every other active worker on the box, including ones for other repos that are doing real work\n4. .ddx/executions/20260429T175957-601d3ad4/prompt.md:3:    <title>agent workers: stale 'running' entries can't be cleaned without server restart</title>
.ddx/executions/20260429T175957-601d3ad4/prompt.md:5:When a local execute-bead/execute-loop process exits abnormally (killed, OOM, parent shell terminated, container crash) the worker entry can remain in the in-memory registry forever. `ddx agent workers` lists it as state=running with elapsed_ms continuously advancing, even though no PID exists.

Reproducing today (axon repo): worker local-20260429T030828-075a28c5 (bead hx-5f4d4ea1, harness=agent, model=qwen3.6) started at 03:08 UTC, has logged elapsed=10+ hours, and `ps -ef | grep execute-bead` shows nothing. The git worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-hx-5f4d4ea1-* has only the checkpoint commit (no actual work commits), confirming the worker died early.

Available paths to clean up are all unsatisfying:
1. `ddx agent workers stop --bead hx-5f4d4ea1` returns 'no matching workers' (because the matcher only finds live workers)
2. `ddx agent workers stop <worker-id>` returns server error 400 'worker not running' (same contradiction — the registry says running, the stopper says not running)
3. `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` works but kills every other active worker on the box, including ones for other repos that are doing real work
4. Manual edit of server-state.json risks corruption and isn't documented

The bead itself wasn't blocked (status=open, owner=null) so the registry noise is mostly cosmetic — it pollutes `ddx agent workers` output and confuses operators trying to triage the queue. But over weeks of use these accumulate (saw 14+ stale entries from prior sessions on this same box, with ages 134h–268h).
.ddx/executions/20260429T175957-601d3ad4/prompt.md:8:AC1. `ddx agent workers prune` (or equivalent) exists and reaps registry entries whose recorded PID is no longer alive (or whose started_at is older than a configurable threshold). Output lists each reaped worker with id, bead_id, harness, age.

AC2. `ddx agent workers stop --bead <id>` succeeds against a stale registry entry too — it should release the registry slot even when the underlying process has already exited. Current 'no matching workers' / 'worker not running' contradiction must be resolved.

AC3. The local-worker registry persists or is reconstructed in a way that survives ddx-server restarts cleanly: either persisted with PID liveness check on restart, or rebuilt from execute-bead/execute-loop on-disk evidence (.ddx/executions/<id>/result.json).

AC4. `ddx agent workers --json` includes a 'liveness' or 'pid_alive' field per entry so operators can see at a glance which entries are live vs stale, even before pruning.

TC-001 in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` still assumes that visiting `/` renders a Dashboard `h1`. That is stale. After the fixture-backed Playwright harness exists, rewrite only the TC-001 Dashboard tests so they navigate through the fixture node/project route and assert the current UI behavior. - `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` — TC-001 block only, currently around lines 9-57.

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/`; those belong to the harness prerequisite.

.ddx/executions/20260501T041653-b64ff2e8/prompt.md:5:Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.

Backend mutation:
- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult
- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP
- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }
- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.
- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.

Auto-repairable strategies:
- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter
- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)
- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map

UI changes to IntegrityPanel:
- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)
- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change
- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)
- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast
- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback

Failure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).
